preamble: This plan describes rollback procedures for each proposed future action. No actions have been executed. All rollback procedures assume the cleanup was performed in a single commit so that git revert or git checkout can restore cleanly.

rollback_entries.proposed_future_command: git rm temp_audit/ambiguity_resolver_report.md && git commit -m 'lapidarium F5: remove temp_audit/ambiguity_resolver_report.md'
rollback_entries.rollback_command: git revert HEAD  OR  git checkout <commit-before-rm> -- temp_audit/ambiguity_resolver_report.md
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: Low — file is preserved in git history

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator approval F5-004 received AND operator has verified file contents are safe to remove
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: Low — but verify no phase gate depends on this file

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator approval F5-005 received AND operator has verified file contents
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: Low — verify no phase gate dependency

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator approval F5-006 received; operator has confirmed TTS code is no longer needed or is preserved in git history
rollback_entries.note: After rollback, file is restored but no tts.py (non-backup) exists — the backup IS the only copy

rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: LOW — legacy/wake/wake.py is the live version

rollback_entries.rollback_command: NOT POSSIBLE via git (file was untracked). Requires manual restoration from operator backup if needed.
rollback_entries.rollback_complexity: IRREVERSIBLE — untracked file; no git history
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: LOW — file is an anomalous CLI fragment with no legitimate use detected

rollback_entries.rollback_command: NOT POSSIBLE via git (untracked). Requires local backup from operator if needed.
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: VERY LOW — invalid WAV format debug artifact

rollback_entries.rollback_command: NOT POSSIBLE via git (untracked). Operator may retain a local copy if needed.

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator approval F5-011 received; careful shell quoting verified
rollback_entries.rollback_command: git revert HEAD  OR  git checkout <commit-before-rm> -- '[:alnum:] \n \'
rollback_entries.rollback_complexity: MEDIUM — requires careful shell quoting in rollback command too
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: VERY LOW — pager artifact with no legitimate use

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator approval F5-012 received; careful shell quoting verified
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: VERY LOW — git command output artifact

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator must explicitly confirm origin and intent via a separate decision prompt BEFORE this item can be authorized

rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: MEDIUM — if this was intentional reference material, loss would be irreversible

rollback_entries.precondition: Operator must confirm origin and intent via separate decision prompt BEFORE authorization
rollback_entries.rollback_command: NOT POSSIBLE via git (untracked 7.0MB PostScript). Operator must retain backup.

rollback_entries.proposed_future_command: SUPPLY CHAIN DECISION REQUIRED — command depends on operator choice
rollback_entries.precondition: Operator must select integration strategy (submodule / vendor / remove / quarantine) via separate decision prompt
rollback_entries.rollback_command: Depends on chosen strategy; removal would be irreversible for untracked files
rollback_entries.rollback_complexity: HIGH — 42 files including a nested .git; removal touches multiple untracked files
rollback_entries.residual_risk_if_not_rolled_back: LOW — the directory is untracked, so it doesn't affect git state
